narrative:
On June 8, 2026, the Karnataka High Court registered an overall hearing rate of 41.0%, resolving 1,316 of the 3,209 scheduled cases across 46 judges. This performance reflects a minor decrease from the 41.4% hearing rate observed on June 5, and continues a downward trajectory from earlier in the week, which saw rates of 46.2% on June 4 and 51.1% on June 3. This decline in conversion efficiency coincided with a substantial expansion in total docket volume, which rose from 2,497 scheduled cases on June 5 to over 3,200 today.

Individual judicial workloads and conversion rates varied widely. Justice S Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the single largest list of 334 scheduled cases, converting 110 (32.9%) into active hearings. Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 handled a massive combined load of 416 cases across three dockets, hearing 189 in total (45.4%). High clearance efficiency was demonstrated by Justice E.S. Indiresh, who heard 60 out of 78 scheduled cases (76.9%), whereas Justice Rajesh Rai K in Hall 39 heard none of his 144 scheduled cases.

In terms of hearing stages, cases listed for "Orders" showed the most progress, with 535 out of 970 cases heard (55.2%). Conversely, "Final Hearings" remained a notable bottleneck, converting just 18.1% of its 741 scheduled matters. By case type, Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the largest category with 1,374 scheduled and 530 heard (38.6%). Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) achieved a higher conversion rate of 50.0% (245 heard of 490), while Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) saw 175 of its 463 scheduled cases heard (37.8%).
